# doit_cloud_diagrams_relationships (Data Source)

Retrieves resource relationships for a Cloud Diagram component by traversing the diagram graph.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Retrieve resource relationships for a Cloud Diagram component.
# This example searches for a node and walks both edge and group-membership
# relationships one hop out from it.

data "doit_cloud_diagrams_search" "lookup" {
  query = "peer"
}

locals {
  # Pick the first node-type component from search results.
  nodes     = [for c in data.doit_cloud_diagrams_search.lookup.component : c if c.type == "node"]
  layer_id  = local.nodes[0].ss_id
  anchor_id = local.nodes[0]._id
}

data "doit_cloud_diagrams_relationships" "example" {
  id        = local.layer_id
  rid       = local.anchor_id
  direction = "both"
  depth     = "direct"
  kind      = "both"
}

output "anchor_name" {
  value = data.doit_cloud_diagrams_relationships.example.anchor.name
}

output "relations_count" {
  value = length(data.doit_cloud_diagrams_relationships.example.relations)
}

output "truncated" {
  value = data.doit_cloud_diagrams_relationships.example.truncated
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) Diagram (layer) ID.
- `rid` (String) Anchor resource ID — must exist as a node, element, or group on the diagram.

### Optional

- `depth` (String) `direct` returns one-hop neighbours; `transitive` runs a cycle-safe BFS to the
connected component (still capped at 200 relations). Defaults to `direct`.
Possible values: `direct`, `transitive`
- `direction` (String) Edge direction relative to the anchor. `both` returns upstream and downstream neighbours. Defaults to `both`.
Possible values: `downstream`, `upstream`, `both`
- `kind` (String) `edges` walks the link graph; `group_members` walks group membership (children
and parent groups); `both` merges the two and dedupes by id, preserving the
smallest observed hop count. Defaults to `edges`.
Possible values: `edges`, `group_members`, `both`
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `anchor` (Attributes) The anchor resource the traversal started from. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--anchor))
- `relations` (Attributes List) Resources related to the anchor (capped at 200). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--relations))
- `truncated` (Boolean) True when the relation list was capped at 200 entries.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `read` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).


<a id="nestedatt--anchor"></a>
### Nested Schema for `anchor`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Anchor resource ID (matches the `rid` path parameter).
- `name` (String) Human-readable resource name.
- `service_type` (String) Cloud service type when available (e.g. `Amazon RDS`, `GCP Cloud SQL`).
- `type` (String) Component bucket the anchor lives in.


<a id="nestedatt--relations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `relations`

Read-Only:

- `hops` (Number) Edge hop count from the anchor. Group-member / group-parent entries are reported as 1.
- `id` (String) Related resource ID.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name of the related resource.
- `relation` (String) How the resource is related to the anchor. `downstream` / `upstream` are edge
relations (anchor is the edge origin / destination respectively).
`group_member` means the related resource is contained in the anchor (anchor is
a group); `group_parent` means the related resource is a group that contains
the anchor.
- `service_type` (String) Cloud service type when available.
- `type` (String) Component bucket the related resource lives in.
